questions.question: What is the difference between a Name Service and a Directory Service? (2 marks)
expectedPoints: A Name Service (e.g., DNS) primarily maps names to addresses for simple lookup.
A Directory Service stores rich attribute information about objects and supports complex search queries based on those attributes.

questions.question: Explain the LDAP Search operation and the role of a search filter. Provide a example filter. (5 marks)
expectedPoints: The Search operation is used to query and retrieve entries from the directory.
It requires a base DN, a scope (base, one-level, subtree), and a search filter.
The search filter defines the criteria an entry must meet to be returned in the results.
Example filter: (&(objectClass=person)(l=London)) which finds person objects located in London.
The operation returns a list of entries matching the filter criteria within the specified scope.
